mentions.note: Cramer pressing bet ahead of quarter; CEO Lisa Su 'is the best.' Expects meaningful upside surprise from data-center momentum and AI chip strength.
Energy-storage SPAC; Cramer approves—'makes a lot of sense.' Reiterates book guidance: hold one SPAC per five-stock diversified portfolio.
Data-center connectivity specialist; Cramer pressing bet alongside Lumentum as semis-on-fire thesis deepens.
$900B valuation in latest funding round, seeking new raise. Combined mega-IPO wave could suck $375B+ from market in span of months—7-8 years of normal IPO volume compressed.
Designs CPU architecture for most chips; now making CPUs itself—could be romper stock. Up big, then reversal in afternoon; not ideal entry but watch closely.
Fabulous semiconductor company focused on data-center connectivity; Cramer quips 'it goes up.' Pressing bet with confidence.
Physical AI/robotics spec play; Pittsburgh team, credible, but path to profitability unclear. Worthy for spec portion of portfolio (1–2 of 10–20 stocks).
Largest US IPO ever (2014) at $120, crashed to $57 by Sept 2015, now at $131—nearly 12 years to recover IPO-peak gains. Historical warning for mega-IPO pop-and-drop dynamics.
Serial upside surpriser; 'Annihilator' coffee best Cramer has had. Small-cap coffee play with cult following; could pop 3 days on beat.
Strong Q1 beat (30¢ earnings, 310 bps tariff absorption), gross margin resilient. Europe exploding (France, UK); US turnaround underway via whimsy, influencer partnerships, and product innovation.
Heavily shorted, building new data centers aggressively. Potential financing/convertible deal could pop stock; watch for CEO announcement (Mike Linton Traeger).
Pressing bet on good quarter; CEO David Joiner fixing company while Rite Aid closed and Walgreens self-immolates. Aetna buried within likely solid too.
Microcosm of higher-end travel market; strong consumer holding should yield good quarter. Fresh start under new CEO Josh Silverman.

Glass and fiber company winning multi-billion-dollar data-center contracts. Analyst meeting Wednesday could move stock higher.
Fifth-largest US IPO; rough first year but CEO Mary Barra executed amazing turnaround with strong buyback. Long-term winner from terrible start.
1-year return +399% (vs S&P +29%). Semis rebound and competitive reset driving recovery; patience on beaten-down names rewarded.
Data-center connectivity enabler helping machines communicate; Cramer bullish on semis-related ecosystem plays.
Always surprises upside; value package resonating, competition weakened. Stock drifting lower despite fundamentals—buy here.
Fourth-largest US IPO (Facebook); cut in half first few months (mobile transition problem), but became incredible long-term stock within months of IPO. Patience paid off.
Reported really good quarter, fell on pharma/healthcare sector sentiment; CEO Rob Davis maneuvered well. Stock recovering (up $3 today). Cramer pressing bet after caller inquiry.

Content delivery and cyber defense against hyperscaler pilfer. Consistent earnings winner reporting after close; terrific defender with strong business model.

Stock went parabolic after reporting this week; auto-linked semi showing strength similar to ON Semiconductor; data-center momentum lifting sector.

$852B valuation after $122B fundraising round (largest private raise in history). IPO capital raise combined with SpaceX/Anthropic creates unprecedented liquidity drain risk.
Stuck in multi-year rut; portfolio cheap with good yield, but no needle-moving catalysts evident. Waiting for upside surprise.

Seventh-largest US IPO (Nov 2021 at $78); ran to $179 in week, fell below $20 by May 2022. Now $15. Nightmare scenario coinciding with market top; warns of mega-IPO risk.
Solid beat, demand holding strong despite stock down 12% post-earnings—'silliest action of season.' Buy aggressively; aircraft servicing demand real; caller 6-for-6 on picks.
Universal fulfillment enabler for SMBs; stock meandering but expected good numbers. Must monitor when it reports Tuesday.
Summer IPO at $2T+ valuation seeking $75B+ raise (3x largest IPO ever). Massive supply influx threatens market; likely hot pop followed by painful 6-month correction.
